event: Saint John the Evangelist's Vision of the Heavenly Jerusalem
symbols: angel pointing upward to a distant city — the heavenly Jerusalem shown to John in vision
kneeling aged apostle — John in awe before the apocalyptic revelation
distant white city on hilltop — the New Jerusalem descending from heaven
large dark wings of the angel — the supernatural messenger bearing the eschatological vision
description: A vertical composition showing an elderly bearded man in a white robe and red cloak kneeling at lower left, gazing upward in awe. Above and beside him, a young angel with large dark wings and a green cloak leans close, one arm around John and the other gesturing upward toward a distant white city visible at the top of the composition. The angel's expression is tender and guiding. The background shows a luminous sky with warm golden tones. The painting has a dynamic, ascending composition drawing the eye from the kneeling figure to the celestial city above.
theological_description: This painting depicts Revelation 21:10, when an angel carried John 'in the Spirit to a great, high mountain, and showed me the holy city Jerusalem coming down out of heaven from God.' The elderly John, exiled on Patmos, receives the culminating vision of all biblical prophecy: the New Jerusalem, where God will dwell with His people and wipe away every tear (REV.21.3-4). The angel serves as the mediating guide of the apocalyptic vision, as throughout Revelation angelic interpreters explain the mysteries to John. The intimate posture of the angel supporting and directing John reflects the pastoral care with which God reveals His ultimate purposes. The distant holy city represents the fulfillment of all God's promises — the garden city where the tree of life grows (REV.22.2), undoing the exile from Eden and establishing the eternal communion between Creator and creation.
